phrases.intent_en: Ask about the old town character
phrases.cultural_note: Monaco-Ville (also called Le Rocher) is the old town — perched on a rocky promontory above the harbor. It's quieter and more residential than Monte-Carlo.

responses.phonetic: wee, suh-LAH ruh-MON-tah oon pay-ree-OHD ahn-SYEN
responses.french: Monaco-Ville est un quartier historique et pittoresque, avec des ruelles étroites et une ambiance calme, loin de l'agitation de Monte-Carlo.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Prince's palace location
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le Palais Princier dans Monaco-Ville ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo seh TROOV luh pah-LAY preen-SYAY don moh-nah-KOH-veel
phrases.cultural_note: The Palais Princier sits at the highest point of Monaco-Ville. It's a short walk from the Place du Palais, where the guard changing ceremony happens.

responses.english: The Prince’s palace is right at the top of the Rock.
responses.phonetic: luh pah-LAY prahn-see-AY eh toot ahn oh doo roh-SHAY
responses.french: Le palais princier est tout en haut du Rocher.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the oceanographic museum location
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le Musée Océanographique ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo seh TROOV luh moo-ZAY oh-say-ah-noh-gra-FEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The Musée Océanographique is on the cliffside below Monaco-Ville — a dramatic location with views over the Mediterranean.

responses.english: The Oceanographic Museum is at the end of the esplanade.
responses.phonetic: luh myoo-ZAY oh-say-ah-noh-grah-FEEK eh oh boo duh les-plah-NAHD
responses.french: Le musée océanographique est au bout de l’esplanade.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the elevator access to the rock
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un ascenseur pour monter au Rocher ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee-ah-TEEL un ah-sahn-SUR poor mohn-TAY oh roh-SHAY
phrases.cultural_note: The 'Ascenseur Public' (public elevator) connects the harbour to Monaco-Ville — free and very useful for avoiding the steep climb.

responses.english: Yes, the elevator is located near the garden.
responses.french: Oui, l'ascenseur public gratuit se trouve près du port Hercule et monte directement à Monaco-Ville en quelques secondes.
